title: Data Quality vs. Quantity
concept: Dumping raw web text directly degrades model quality; high-quality, curated subsets (like clean textbooks or code) accelerate learning and reasoning.

exercises.prompt: To help an AI learn to think and write clearly, what should we feed it?

options.text: The largest dataset available, regardless of errors.

exercises.explain: AI models learn from the data we give them. If we give them garbage, they output garbage. Clean data is the key to smart AI.

cards.title: Would you study a messy street?
cards.body: If you wanted to learn physics, would you read a carefully written school textbook, or would you read millions of random sticky notes found on a messy street? AI has the same choice! Let's find out why smart data beats big data.

cards.title: What is Data Quality vs. Quantity?
cards.body: Data Quality vs. Quantity is the idea that AI models learn much better from a small amount of clean, structured information (like curated textbooks) than from a massive amount of messy, random web text (like spam or social media arguments).

cards.title: The Giant Leaf Pile vs. Fresh Fruit
cards.body: Imagine eating a massive dump truck full of dry leaves versus a small, delicious bowl of fresh fruit. The pile of leaves is huge (high quantity), but it won't help you grow. The fresh fruit (high quality) is small but packed with vitamins that instantly make you feel stronger and more energetic.

cards.title: Coding Lessons vs. Chat Forums
cards.body: An AI trained on 1 billion words from high-quality coding textbooks can write correct computer programs. Meanwhile, an AI trained on 10 billion words of messy internet comments might just repeat jokes and make syntax errors.

cards.title: Myth: More Data is Always Better
cards.body: People often think that to make a smarter AI, you just need to feed it the entire internet. In reality, raw web text contains so much garbage, spam, and errors that it actually degrades the AI's intelligence and reasoning abilities.

cards.title: Messy Web vs. Clean Books
cards.body: See how the type of data we feed the AI changes what it actually learns.

left.text: Contains spam, typos, and false facts. Makes the AI confused and buggy.

right.text: Contains clean textbooks and code. Makes the AI smart, logical, and fast.

exercises.prompt: What happens when we dump raw, unedited web text directly into an AI's training program?

options.text: The AI automatically ignores all the mistakes.
The AI becomes instantly perfect at everything.
The AI's performance and reasoning quality degrade.
The AI gets physically faster at loading websites.

exercises.explain: Raw web text contains spelling mistakes, false facts, and spam. When an AI reads this, it learns these bad habits, which lowers its output quality.

exercises.prompt: True or False: A giant dataset full of errors is better for training an AI than a small dataset of clean textbooks.
exercises.explain: Quality is much more important than sheer volume. A small, clean dataset helps an AI learn much better than a massive, messy one.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe the best kind of training data for a smart AI:
exercises.sentence: Instead of raw internet text, AI developers prefer using clean, ________ datasets.
exercises.explain: Curated data is carefully selected and cleaned. It acts like a high-quality study guide, helping the AI learn accurate information quickly.

exercises.prompt: Which of these training datasets will make an AI better at logical reasoning?

options.text: Dataset A: 50 million pages of random social media posts and arguments.
Dataset B: 1 million pages of clean math textbooks and coding guides.
Dataset C: 100 million pages of automatic website spam and ads.

exercises.explain: Dataset B consists of curated textbooks, which provide structured, correct, and high-quality lessons that help the AI learn how to reason step-by-step.

exercises.prompt: If you want to train an AI to solve tough math problems quickly, which dataset strategy should you choose?

options.text: Feeding the AI as much raw, uncleaned text as possible from any source.
Using smaller, carefully curated, and clean datasets like textbooks.
Increasing the size of the AI model without changing the training data.
Allowing the AI to read spelling errors so it learns to write badly.

exercises.explain: More data isn't always better. Unfiltered web scrapings introduce errors, biases, and nonsense, which degrades performance. Clean, high-quality, curated datasets act like premium learning materials, helping the model learn reasoning faster and with fewer parameters.
